name: Nicolae Picu
description:
'''Nicolae Picu''' (Romanian pronunciation: [<nowiki/>[[Help:IPA/Romanian|nikoˈla.e ˈpiku]]]; also known as {{Lang|ro|Moș Neculai}}; 1789, [[Suceava]] – 2 October 1864, Suceava) was a Romanian [[Lăutari|lăutar]] violinist from the [[Duchy of Bukovina]] then part of the [[Austrian Empire]].

He played a significant role in bringing traditional folk melodies to professional musicians of international stature, such as [[Franz Liszt]] and [[Karol Mikuli]], thereby introducing Romanian [[lăutărească music]] to the academic musical world of his time.
